input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI targeting Android + Windows on Windows 11.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest stable automatically)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover current MAUI workload name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install the workload
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installation
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows targets require the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling, which come via VS components:

```powershell
# Install VS 2022 Build Tools with required workloads
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Then modify the install to add required components:
# (or include them upfront with --add flags)
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --passive --norestart
```

## 4. Install Android SDK & Java (Android target)

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK pieces, but you can also manage them explicitly:

```powershell
# Check what the MAUI workload already provisioned
dotnet workload list

# Install OpenJDK (required by Android tooling)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Set JAVA_HOME
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17", "User")

# --- Android SDK via command-line tools ---
# If not already installed by VS, install manually:
winget install Google.AndroidStudio  # optional, or just the CLI tools

# Or download cmdline-tools directly:
$sdkRoot = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $sdkRoot, "User")

# Discover available platform/build-tool versions dynamically
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -First 10

& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "build-tools;" | Select-Object -First 10

# Install the versions you need (example: latest API + build tools)
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Verify the Full Toolchain

```powershell
# Built-in MAUI doctor command — checks everything at once
dotnet workload run maui-check 2>$null

# If maui-check isn't available, use the community tool:
dotnet tool install -g Redth.Net.Maui.Check
maui-check
```

This validates: .NET SDK, MAUI workload, Android SDK, Java, Windows App SDK.

## 6. Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Discover available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## 7. (Optional) Android Emulator Setup

```powershell
# List available emulator images
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "system-images;android-"

# Install an image
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" "emulator"

# Create an AVD
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API_35" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel"

# Launch it
& "$sdkRoot\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_API_35
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0-*` with `net10.0-*` if you're on .NET 10 (check `dotnet --version`).
- The `--list` flags on `sdkmanager` and `dotnet workload search` are how you **dynamically discover** correct versions instead of hardcoding them.
- `maui-check` is the single best validation tool — run it after any change.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They want PowerShell commands and dynamic version discovery. This is a knowledge/advice question - I don't need to search a codebase or run commands. I'll provide a comprehensive plan.
